message:
perf(btree,pager): cache parent page hint for zero-traversal quick-balance splits and defer :memory: retained-commit page flushes

Btree cursor changes (cursor.rs):
- Add `parent_page: Option<PageNumber>` to RightmostLeafCacheEntry and
  TableAppendHint so that the cached rightmost-leaf fast-append path can
  remember the interior parent page it last traversed through
- Introduce `current_parent_page_hint_from_stack()` helper that reads
  the parent page number from the cursor stack without a separate tree
  walk
- Add `try_quick_balance_on_external_rightmost_leaf_hint()` which uses
  the cached parent page to call balance_quick_known_divider_rowid
  directly, avoiding a full cursor.last() traversal when the rightmost
  leaf fills up and triggers a page split — this eliminates O(depth)
  page reads on every split during sequential bulk inserts
- Wire parent_page propagation through all insert, split, and cache
  refresh paths so the hint stays accurate across the lifetime of a
  sequential append burst
- Add test exercising the post-root-split cached-hint split path to
  verify that only the parent page is read (not the full tree)

Pager changes (pager.rs):
- Add `retained_committed_pages_unflushed` flag to SimpleTransaction to
  track when a :memory: single-connection retained writer has deferred
  its backing-storage flush
- Introduce `retain_committed_pages_in_txn_read_cache()` which drains
  the write set into the transaction-local read cache instead of
  flushing to MemoryVfs backing storage, so metadata-only autocommit
  statements amortize the page-copy cost across the full burst
- Introduce `flush_retained_committed_pages_to_storage()` which
  batch-writes all deferred txn_read_cache pages to the MemoryVfs
  backing file in a single write_page_batch call when the retained
  writer is finally released or when a non-metadata-only commit forces
  a flush
- Add txn_read_cache lookup in the single-connection read path so that
  retained committed pages are served from the authoritative
  transaction-local cache instead of the stale shared cache
- Wire flush points into commit, rollback, and retain_commit so deferred
  pages are never silently lost
- Expand tests to verify publication plane stays empty during retained
  commits, visible_commit_seq still advances, txn_read_cache contains
  authoritative page data, stale shared cache is bypassed, and final
  commit flushes deferred pages to backing storage

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
